title: lithocardium hispidum
intro: Lithocardium hispidum, also known as Digitalis purpurea or Foxglove, is a flowering plant species native to Europe, western Asia, and North Africa. The plant contains cardenolides, compounds with medicinal properties, which have been used to treat various heart conditions, including atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. Lithocardium hispidum's health benefits include increased cardiac output, reduced heart rate, and improved overall cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Lithocardium hispidum are typically made from the leaves and are often used to treat heart conditions, with caution and under medical supervision due to potential toxicity.
